meaning: n.鼓槌,鼓槌形物体(如鸡腿等)
examples: Chest meat, he asserted, is too stringy, whereas the drumstick of a young chicken is delicate and easy to chew.
The first dish was roasted chicken. Immediately, the old man took the drumstick for the old lady.
Then the only thing to fight about will be who gets the last drumstick.
Or that undisciplined mook holding a dripping drumstick in one hand and a pint of ice cream and soda pop in the other?
